steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Apply similarity to identify equal angles
steps.content: Triangle $CAE \sim$ triangle $CBD$, with the vertex correspondence $C\leftrightarrow C$, $A\leftrightarrow B$, $E\leftrightarrow D$. Corresponding angles in similar triangles are congruent, so angle $CAE$ = angle $CBD$. The side scale factor ($AE = 28 \cdot BD$) does not affect angle measures.

steps.title: Find the requested value
steps.content:
Since angle $CBD = 59^{\circ}$:
$$\angle CAE = \angle CBD = 59^{\circ}$$
That is the correct answer.
